oneLiner: PEP is a light, social protein drink designed for lively nights out, rooftop gatherings, and feel-good occasions—delivering premium taste and festival-ready vibes without the alcohol.
positioningStatement: For sociable, aesthetic-driven people who love to go out, PEP replaces alcohol and boring soft drinks with a delicious, vibrant drink that makes every social moment feel like a celebration.
audience: Urban, social adults (18-35) who want a premium non-alcoholic option
Nightlife and dinner-goers looking for a better ritual than sugary drinks
Design-conscious consumers seeking an Instagram-worthy, fun experience
Groups, friends, and date nights seeking an alternative to alcohol
occasions: Bars and restaurants as an alcohol replacement

voice.tone: Playful, confident, experiential, celebratory, never childish, never gym-bro.

visual.mood: Vibrant, premium, fun, festive—evoking sunset parties and new rituals.
visual.photography: People socializing—closeups with hands, sparkling drinks, natural but bold lighting; vibrant fruit; real, stylish settings (not sterile or gym-like).
visual.typography: Didot for logo (elegant & chic), Brittany Signature for tagline/script, Montserrat for body/labels; lean on bold, expressive type and handwritten signature details for personality.
visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity

inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: A leading non-alcoholic social drink built for nightlife rituals; leans into experiential, mood-enhancing and fun occasions.

inLineBrands.name: Lyre's
inLineBrands.why: Elegant, spirit-like branding for non-alcoholic drinks. Stands out as a premium ritual drink for sophisticated social moments.

inLineBrands.name: Seedlip
inLineBrands.why: Category-definer for high-end, social, alcohol-free experiences; packaging and product ritual mirror premium spirits and cocktails.
designPatterns: focus on occasion and social setting

positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: PROTOCHOL
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Blends the social ritual of drinking with the protein cue, targeting a similar social mindset (out, experimental, fun).
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Contains alcohol and leans toward a hybrid protein/alcohol concept, which is a fundamental mismatch (PEP is non-alcoholic).

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional vitamin drink for health optimization—lacks social, premium ritual focus; belongs in the gym/health lane, not nightlife.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Electrolyte/hydration product targeting performance and sports use. Design and occasion are clinical, not festive or social.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: BCAA/energy drink for sports/recovery; gym and performance-centric, not sociable or experiential.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Vitamin hydration for wellness, not ritual or celebration. Design fits functional context, not lively social scenes.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic soda for daily wellness; too functional and lifestyle-health-focused, missing the social/festival edge.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Non-alc aperitif for wellness upgrades; beautiful but more suited to natural/health moments than vibrant social energy.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Health-first, digestive-focused soda; appeal and branding are for everyday wellness, not out-of-home nightlife.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Wellness/health soda for the functional lifestyle crowd. Bright but lacks that special occasion, out-of-home focus.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Kombucha for wellness and daily hydration. Clean design, but the occasion is everyday, not nightlife or events.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Relaxation, chill, soft wellness, and stress-relief as core brand. Supportive for daytime, not the energy or buzz of social night.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Ready cocktails (with alcohol focus); main vibe is convenience with a festive edge, but alcohol is central, which is not PEP.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Alcohol-free spirits but positioned for mindful, lifestyle upgrades rather than out-and-out vibrant revelry.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / generic gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Fitness-bro, gym-centric, nutrition-driven: not for social occasions, alienates the target 'fun' audience.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com (US energy/probiotic)
antiReferences.why: Name collision—entirely different product; probiotic/energy focus and branding in US. Do NOT reference.

antiReferences.name: NOCCO
antiReferences.why: Too sporty, performance branding and BCAA focus—wrong occasion, wrong vibe.

brandFitRules: If the core occasion isn’t social/group, it’s NOT a fit.
If visual cues are gym, clinical, supplement, or childlike, REJECT.
If alcohol is central to the value prop, REJECT.
Design must balance premium with vibrant/fun; reject both sterile and juvenile.
Brand voice = playful, confident, and celebratory; never medical, preachy, or fitness-jargon.
Adjectives must reference fun, social, premium, vibrant—not wellness, hydration, or discipline.
Photography and visual identity must show real social occasions, not product on white, sports, or yoga poses.
Avoid minimalist wellness branding (white, pastel, generic). Must have arresting, bold, colorful feel.
